schools.summary: The LORD speaking to Moses concerning the setting up of the tabernacle: 'and the LORD spake unto Moses, saying' — the LORD speaking to Moses, now to command the rearing up of the tabernacle (vv.2-16), all the parts being made and ready (chs. 36-39, the speaking, God's command, the word that orders the setting up, the occasion, the tabernacle complete, now to be erected and consecrated); the LORD speaking (the command to set up the tabernacle) — the divine authority (the rearing of the tabernacle by God's command, not man's invention, the order, God directing every step); the LORD spake unto Moses — the command to rear the tabernacle (vv.2, the providence, God ordering the setting up of his dwelling, the word that commands the work, the figure of God's house reared by his word) — Henry, Gill.

themes: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying
the LORD speaking to Moses concerning the setting up of the tabernacle
God ordering the rearing of his dwelling by his word (vv.2-16)
the work directed by God himself, not man's invention

schools.summary: The command to rear the tabernacle on the first day of the first month: 'on the first day of the first month shalt thou set up the tabernacle of the tent of the congregation' — the time — 'on the first day of the first month shalt thou set up the tabernacle of the tent of the congregation': the tabernacle to be reared on the first day of the first month (Abib/Nisan), the beginning of the second year after the Exodus (vv.17, the witnesses noting the significance — the new beginning, the dwelling of God set up at the turn of the year, the first day, the new start, almost a year after the Exodus, Exodus 12:2; 19:1, the dwelling of God among men, the tabernacle the dwelling-place of God in the midst of his people, the great purpose, 'let them make me a sanctuary; that I may dwell among them,' Exodus 25:8, the figure — God coming to dwell with man, the tabernacle a figure of the incarnation, 'the Word was made flesh, and dwelt [tabernacled] among us,' John 1:14, and of God's dwelling with his people, the church, Ephesians 2:21-22, and the consummation, Revelation 21:3, 'the tabernacle of God is with men, and he will dwell with them,' the new creation, the dwelling reared at the new year, the figure of the new beginning, God dwelling with man); the tent of the congregation (the place of meeting, where God met his people) — the meeting-place (God dwelling and meeting with his people, the figure, the dwelling and meeting of God with man, fulfilled in Christ); the tabernacle set up on the first day — the dwelling reared (the providence, the dwelling of God set up, the figure of God coming to dwell with man) — Calvin, Wesley, Keil, Gill.

debates.question: What is the significance of the rearing of the tabernacle?

sides.position: The dwelling of God among his people, reared at the new beginning of the year (Exodus 25:8; 12:2) - a figure of God coming to dwell with man, the incarnation ('the Word tabernacled among us,' John 1:14) and God's dwelling with his church and at the consummation (Ephesians 2:21-22; Revelation 21:3)

themes: On the first day of the first month shalt thou set up the tabernacle of the tent of the congregation
the command to rear the tabernacle on the first day of the first month (the crux of the dwelling of God set up)
the dwelling of God among his people, reared at the new beginning (Exodus 25:8; 12:2)
a figure of God coming to dwell with man in Christ (John 1:14; Revelation 21:3)

schools.summary: The command to place the ark and screen it with the veil: 'and thou shalt put therein the ark of the testimony, and cover the ark with the vail' — the ark (the holiest object) to be placed in the most holy place, and screened with the veil (the ark first, the holiest furniture set in place first, the heart of the tabernacle, the veil, covering, screening the ark in the most holy place, dividing it from the holy place, Exodus 26:33, the figure, the ark hidden behind the veil, the holiest place not yet open, Hebrews 9:8); the ark set in the most holy place (the throne of God, the chest of the law, Exodus 25:16) — the order (the holiest first, the center of the dwelling); 'cover the ark with the vail' (the veil screening the ark, the most holy place divided, Exodus 26:33, the figure, the way into the holiest not yet made manifest, the veil yet standing, Hebrews 9:8; 10:19-20, the veil rent in Christ, the providence, the ark veiled in the most holy place, the figure of the hidden holiest opened in Christ) — Ellicott, Gill.

themes: And thou shalt put therein the ark of the testimony, and cover the ark with the vail
the command to place the ark and screen it with the veil
the holiest furniture set first, screened by the veil (Exodus 26:33)
the figure of the hidden holiest opened in Christ (Hebrews 9:8; 10:19-20)

schools.summary: The command to set the table and light the candlestick: 'and thou shalt bring in the table, and set in order the things that are to be set in order upon it; and thou shalt bring in the candlestick, and light the lamps thereof' — the table (with its shewbread) and the candlestick (with its lamps lit) to be set in the holy place (the table, the bread of the Presence set in order, Exodus 25:30, the candlestick, the lamps lit, the light of the holy place, Exodus 25:37, the figures, Christ the bread, the light of the Spirit and the church); the table and the shewbread set in order (the bread of the Presence, the food before God, Exodus 25:30) — the figure (Christ the bread of life, John 6:35); 'bring in the candlestick, and light the lamps thereof' (the candlestick with the lamps lit, the light of the holy place, Exodus 25:37, the figure, the light of the Spirit, the church the light-bearer, Revelation 1:20; Matthew 5:14-16, the providence, the table and candlestick set, the figures of Christ the bread and the light) — Clarke, Gill.

themes: And thou shalt bring in the table, and set in order the things that are to be set in order upon it; and thou shalt bring in the candlestick, and light the lamps thereof
the command to set the table and light the candlestick
the bread of the Presence and the light of the holy place (Exodus 25:30, 37)
the figures of Christ the bread of life and the light (John 6:35; Revelation 1:20)

schools.summary: The command to set the golden altar and the door hanging: 'and thou shalt set the altar of gold for the incense before the ark of the testimony, and put the hanging of the door to the tabernacle' — the golden altar of incense to be set in the holy place, before the veil (before the ark beyond it), and the screen (hanging) put at the door of the tabernacle (the golden altar, the altar of incense, before the veil, Exodus 30:6, the incense, the figure of prayer, Psalm 141:2, the door hanging, the screen at the entrance of the holy place, Exodus 26:36); the golden altar before the veil (the altar of prayer, before the ark beyond the veil, Exodus 30:6) — the figure (prayer ascending before God, the intercession of Christ, Hebrews 7:25; Revelation 8:3-4); 'put the hanging of the door to the tabernacle' (the screen at the door, the entrance to the holy place, Exodus 26:36, the figure, the entrance, the access to the holy place, the providence, the golden altar and the door, the figures of prayer and the entrance) — Ellicott, Gill.

themes: And thou shalt set the altar of gold for the incense before the ark of the testimony, and put the hanging of the door to the tabernacle
the command to set the golden altar and the door hanging
the golden altar of prayer before the veil (Exodus 30:6; Psalm 141:2)
the figures of prayer and Christ's intercession, the entrance screened (Hebrews 7:25; Exodus 26:36)

schools.summary: The command to set the altar of burnt offering before the door: 'and thou shalt set the altar of the burnt offering before the door of the tabernacle of the tent of the congregation' — the brazen altar of burnt offering to be set in the court, before the door of the tabernacle (the altar, the place of sacrifice, at the entrance of the court, Exodus 27:1-8, the position, before the door, the first thing met on entering, the sacrifice the way of approach, the figure, the altar of sacrifice the first thing met, no approach to God but by the altar, by atonement, Hebrews 9:22; 13:10); the altar of burnt offering (the place of sacrifice, vv.38:1) — the figure (the cross, the place of atonement); 'before the door of the tabernacle of the tent of the congregation' (the altar at the entrance, the first thing met, the sacrifice the way of approach, the figure, no approach to God but by the altar of sacrifice, the cross the way, Hebrews 10:19-22, the providence, the altar of sacrifice at the door, the figure of the cross the way of access) — Ellicott, Gill.

themes: And thou shalt set the altar of the burnt offering before the door of the tabernacle of the tent of the congregation
the command to set the altar of burnt offering before the door
the place of sacrifice the first thing met, the way of approach (Exodus 27:1-8)
no approach to God but by the altar, the cross the way of access (Hebrews 10:19-22)

schools.summary: The command to set the laver and fill it with water: 'and thou shalt set the laver between the tent of the congregation and the altar, and shalt put water therein' — the laver to be set between the tabernacle and the altar, and filled with water for washing (the laver, the basin for the priests' washing, Exodus 30:18-21, the position, between the altar and the tent, the washing after the sacrifice, before entering, the water, for the cleansing of the priests, the figure, the washing after the atonement, the cleansing of those who approach, Titus 3:5; Hebrews 10:22); the laver between the altar and the tent (the washing after the sacrifice, before the service, Exodus 30:18-20) — the order (the sacrifice, then the washing, then the approach); 'put water therein' (the water for washing, the cleansing of the priests, the figure, the washing of water by the word, the cleansing of those who serve God, Ephesians 5:26; Hebrews 10:22, 'having our bodies washed with pure water,' the providence, the laver and water, the figure of cleansing after atonement) — JFB, Gill.

themes: And thou shalt set the laver between the tent of the congregation and the altar, and shalt put water therein
the command to set the laver and fill it with water
the washing after the atonement, before the approach (Exodus 30:18-21)
the cleansing of those who serve God (Ephesians 5:26; Hebrews 10:22)

schools.summary: The command to set up the court and its gate: 'and thou shalt set up the court round about, and hang up the hanging at the court gate' — the court (the enclosure) to be set up around the tabernacle, with the hanging (screen) at the gate (the court, the enclosure of white linen, separating the holy from the common, Exodus 27:9-19, the gate, the one entrance into the court, the screen at the gate, the figure, the enclosure of righteousness, the one gate, Christ the door, John 10:9); the court set up (the enclosure of the white linen hangings, Exodus 27:9) — the figure (the separation, the white walls of righteousness, Revelation 19:8); 'hang up the hanging at the court gate' (the screen at the gate, the one entrance, Exodus 27:16, the figure, the one door, Christ the way, John 10:9; 14:6, the providence, the court and gate set up, the enclosure and the one entrance) — Ellicott, Gill.

themes: And thou shalt set up the court round about, and hang up the hanging at the court gate
the command to set up the court and its gate
the enclosure of righteousness, separating the holy from the common (Exodus 27:9-19)
the one gate, the figure of Christ the door (John 10:9; 14:6)

schools.summary: The command to anoint and hallow the tabernacle: 'and thou shalt take the anointing oil, and anoint the tabernacle, and all that is therein, and shalt hallow it, and all the vessels thereof: and it shall be holy' — the tabernacle and all its furniture to be anointed with the holy oil and hallowed (consecrated, set apart as holy, the anointing, the consecration with the holy oil, Exodus 30:26-29, the hallowing, the setting apart, the dedication to God, the holiness, the tabernacle and vessels made holy, devoted wholly to God); the anointing with the holy oil (the consecration, the setting apart) — the figure (the oil of the Spirit, the consecration by the Spirit, the tabernacle, the furniture, the priests all anointed, the figure of the consecration of God's house and people by the Spirit, 1 John 2:20, 27; 1 Corinthians 6:11, the dedication, the whole made holy to God); 'and it shall be holy' (the tabernacle and vessels made holy, set apart, sacred, the figure, the holiness of God's house and people, sanctified by the Spirit, Ephesians 5:26; 1 Peter 2:9, the providence, the anointing and hallowing, the consecration of God's dwelling) — Calvin, Gill, Ellicott.

themes: And thou shalt take the anointing oil, and anoint the tabernacle, and all that is therein, and shalt hallow it, and all the vessels thereof: and it shall be holy
the command to anoint and hallow the tabernacle
the dwelling and its furniture set apart and made holy by the anointing oil (Exodus 30:26-29)
the figure of the consecration of God's house and people by the Spirit (1 John 2:20, 27; 1 Peter 2:9)

schools.summary: The command to bring and wash Aaron and his sons: 'and thou shalt bring Aaron and his sons unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, and wash them with water' — Aaron and his sons to be brought to the door and washed with water, the first act of their consecration to the priesthood (the bringing, the priests presented for consecration, Exodus 29:4, the washing, the cleansing with water, the first step of the consecration, the figure, the washing of those set apart for God's service, the cleansing of the priesthood, Titus 3:5; Hebrews 10:22); Aaron and his sons brought for consecration (the priests presented, Exodus 29:4) — the priesthood (the consecration of the priests to their office); 'wash them with water' (the washing, the cleansing, the first act of consecration, Exodus 29:4, the figure, the washing of regeneration, the cleansing of those set apart, Titus 3:5, the priestly people washed, Revelation 1:5-6, the providence, the priests washed, the figure of the cleansing of God's servants) — Calvin, Gill, Ellicott.

themes: And thou shalt bring Aaron and his sons unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, and wash them with water
the command to bring and wash Aaron and his sons
the priests brought and washed, the first act of consecration (Exodus 29:4)
the figure of the cleansing of God's servants (Titus 3:5; Revelation 1:5-6)

schools.summary: The command to clothe and anoint Aaron as priest: 'and thou shalt put upon Aaron the holy garments, and anoint him, and sanctify him; that he may minister unto me in the priest's office' — Aaron to be clothed with the holy garments, anointed, and sanctified (consecrated) to minister as high priest (the garments, the holy vestments, the glory and beauty, Exodus 28:2, the anointing, the holy oil poured on his head, the consecration, Exodus 29:7; Psalm 133:2, the office, Aaron set apart to minister as high priest, the figure, Christ the great High Priest, clothed and anointed, Hebrews 5:4-5, the anointed One); Aaron clothed and anointed (the high priest consecrated, Exodus 29:5-7) — the figure (Christ the High Priest, anointed with the Spirit, Hebrews 1:9; Acts 10:38); 'that he may minister unto me in the priest's office' (Aaron set apart to minister, the high-priestly office, the figure, the high priest a type of Christ, ministering before God, Hebrews 7:24-25, the providence, Aaron consecrated as high priest, the figure of Christ the great High Priest) — JFB.

themes: And thou shalt put upon Aaron the holy garments, and anoint him, and sanctify him; that he may minister unto me in the priest's office
the command to clothe and anoint Aaron as priest
Aaron clothed, anointed, and consecrated as high priest (Exodus 29:5-7)
the figure of Christ the great High Priest, anointed with the Spirit (Hebrews 1:9; Acts 10:38)

schools.summary: The command to clothe Aaron's sons: 'and thou shalt bring his sons, and clothe them with coats' — Aaron's sons to be brought and clothed with coats (the priestly garments of the common priests, Exodus 28:40, the sons, the common priests, the line of Aaron, the coats, the simpler vestments of the priests, not the high-priestly glory, the figure, the priestly family clothed for service, the priestly people, 1 Peter 2:9; Revelation 1:6); the sons brought (the common priests, the line of Aaron, Exodus 28:40) — the priesthood (the sons clothed for the priestly service); 'clothe them with coats' (the coats, the priestly garments of the sons, Exodus 28:40, the figure, the priestly people clothed for service, a royal priesthood, 1 Peter 2:9, the providence, the sons clothed, the priestly family set apart) — Ellicott.

themes: And thou shalt bring his sons, and clothe them with coats
the command to clothe Aaron's sons
the sons clothed for the priestly service (Exodus 28:40)
the figure of the priestly people clothed for God (1 Peter 2:9; Revelation 1:6)

schools.summary: The anointing unto an everlasting priesthood: 'and thou shalt anoint them, as thou didst anoint their father, that they may minister unto me in the priest's office: for their anointing shall surely be an everlasting priesthood throughout their generations' — Aaron's sons to be anointed as he was, to minister as priests, their anointing constituting an everlasting (perpetual) priesthood throughout their generations (the anointing, the consecration of the sons, Exodus 30:30, the everlasting priesthood, the Aaronic priesthood perpetual, descending through the generations); the everlasting priesthood — 'their anointing shall surely be an everlasting priesthood throughout their generations': the Aaronic priesthood perpetual, descending through Aaron's line (the witnesses noting the meaning — the priesthood continuing as long as the old covenant stood, the perpetual Aaronic line, Numbers 25:13, the figure — the everlasting priesthood ultimately fulfilled in Christ, the true and eternal High Priest, the Aaronic priesthood a shadow, passing, but pointing to Christ's unchangeable, everlasting priesthood, Hebrews 7:23-25, 'this man, because he continueth ever, hath an unchangeable priesthood,' Hebrews 7:17, 'thou art a priest for ever after the order of Melchisedec,' the one everlasting Priest, the contrast, the many Aaronic priests, dying and succeeding, vs. the one ever-living Christ, the priesthood the means of access to God, perpetual in Christ); 'throughout their generations' (the priesthood descending through the generations, the perpetual line, the figure, the everlasting priesthood, fulfilled in the eternal Priest, Christ, the providence, the Aaronic priesthood perpetual, the figure of Christ's everlasting priesthood) — Clarke, Wesley, Gill.

sides.position: The Aaronic priesthood perpetual through the generations (Numbers 25:13) - a figure of the everlasting priesthood fulfilled in Christ, the one ever-living High Priest with an unchangeable priesthood (Hebrews 7:23-25; 7:17), the perpetual means of access to God

themes: And thou shalt anoint them, as thou didst anoint their father... for their anointing shall surely be an everlasting priesthood throughout their generations
the anointing unto an everlasting priesthood (the crux of the perpetual priesthood)
the Aaronic priesthood perpetual through the generations (Numbers 25:13)
a figure of the everlasting priesthood fulfilled in Christ the eternal High Priest (Hebrews 7:23-25, 17)

schools.summary: Moses doing all according to the LORD's command: 'thus did Moses: according to all that the LORD commanded him, so did he' — Moses doing exactly as the LORD commanded, in all the setting up and consecration of the tabernacle (the obedience, Moses doing all according to the command, nothing omitted or altered, the refrain, the repeated note of exact obedience, 'as the LORD commanded Moses,' running through chs. 39-40, the faithfulness, Moses the faithful servant, doing all God's will, Hebrews 3:5); Moses doing all as commanded (exactly, fully, nothing of his own, the pattern followed, Exodus 25:40) — the faithfulness (the faithful servant, doing all God's will, the lesson, the work of God done in exact obedience, not by human invention); 'so did he' (Moses doing all, the full obedience, the work completed as commanded, the figure, the faithful servant, Hebrews 3:5, the obedience of faith, the providence, Moses' exact obedience, the work of God's house done according to the pattern) — Henry, Gill, Ellicott.

themes: Thus did Moses: according to all that the LORD commanded him, so did he
Moses doing all according to the LORD's command
the work of God's house done in exact obedience, nothing altered (Exodus 25:40)
Moses the faithful servant doing all God's will (Hebrews 3:5)

schools.summary: The rearing of the tabernacle in the second year: 'and it came to pass in the first month in the second year, on the first day of the month, that the tabernacle was reared up' — the tabernacle reared up on the first day of the first month of the second year (Nisan/Abib), as commanded (vv.2, the day, the first of the year, the new beginning, the second year, almost a year after the Exodus, Exodus 12:2; 19:1, the rearing, the dwelling of God set up); the first day of the first month of the second year (the new year, the dwelling reared, about nine months after the giving of the law, Exodus 19) — the timing (the dwelling set up at the turn of the year, the new beginning); 'the tabernacle was reared up' (the dwelling of God set up, the tabernacle erected, the dwelling among men, the figure, God coming to dwell, John 1:14, the providence, the tabernacle reared, the dwelling of God established among his people) — Calvin, Gill, Ellicott.

themes: And it came to pass in the first month in the second year, on the first day of the month, that the tabernacle was reared up
the rearing of the tabernacle in the second year
the dwelling of God set up at the new beginning of the year (Exodus 12:2; 19:1)
the figure of God coming to dwell with man (John 1:14)

schools.summary: Moses rearing up the framework of the tabernacle: 'and Moses reared up the tabernacle, and fastened his sockets, and set up the boards thereof, and put in the bars thereof, and reared up his pillars' — Moses setting up the framework: the sockets (bases), boards, bars, and pillars (Exodus 26, the rearing, Moses erecting the structure, the framework of the dwelling, the sockets, the silver bases, the foundation of the atonement money, vv.38:27, the boards and bars, the framework joined together, the pillars, the supports of the veil and the door); the sockets fastened (the silver bases, the foundation, Exodus 26:19) — the foundation (the structure resting on the silver of redemption, vv.38:27); 'set up the boards thereof, and put in the bars thereof, and reared up his pillars' (the boards, bars, and pillars, the framework of the dwelling, Exodus 26, the figure, the structure of God's house, every part joined, the building fitly framed, Ephesians 2:21, the providence, the framework reared, the dwelling of God built up) — Keil and Delitzsch, Gill.

themes: And Moses reared up the tabernacle, and fastened his sockets, and set up the boards thereof, and put in the bars thereof, and reared up his pillars
Moses rearing up the framework of the tabernacle
the framework of God's dwelling reared and joined, resting on the silver foundation (Exodus 26; vv.38:27)
the building fitly framed together (Ephesians 2:21)

schools.summary: The spreading of the tent and its covering: 'and he spread abroad the tent over the tabernacle, and put the covering of the tent above upon it; as the LORD commanded Moses' — Moses spreading the tent (the curtains of goats' hair) over the tabernacle, and the covering (the skins) above it, as the LORD commanded (the tent, the goats'-hair curtains, the covering over the linen curtains, Exodus 26:7, the covering, the skins, the outer covering, Exodus 26:14, the refrain, 'as the LORD commanded Moses,' the exact obedience repeated); the tent spread over (the goats'-hair curtains, the covering, Exodus 26:7) — the protection (the coverings over the dwelling); 'as the LORD commanded Moses' (the refrain of exact obedience, Moses doing all as commanded, the repeated note through the chapter, the figure, the faithful obedience, the work done as God appointed, the providence, the covering spread, the obedience repeated, the dwelling covered as commanded) — Clarke, Gill.

themes: And he spread abroad the tent over the tabernacle, and put the covering of the tent above upon it; as the LORD commanded Moses
the spreading of the tent and its covering
the coverings spread over the dwelling (Exodus 26:7, 14)
the work done in exact obedience, 'as the LORD commanded Moses'

schools.summary: The placing of the testimony in the ark and the mercy seat above: 'and he took and put the testimony into the ark, and set the staves on the ark, and put the mercy seat above upon the ark' — Moses putting the testimony (the tables of the law) into the ark, setting the staves, and putting the mercy seat on top (the testimony, the tables of the law, the covenant, placed in the ark, Exodus 25:16, the mercy seat, the propitiatory placed above the law, mercy over the law, the figure, the law within, covered by the mercy seat, the law hidden under mercy, the propitiation over the broken law, Romans 3:25; Psalm 85:10); the testimony (the tables of the law) put into the ark (the law within, Exodus 25:16) — the figure (the law in the heart of Christ, Psalm 40:8; Hebrews 10:7); 'put the mercy seat above upon the ark' (the mercy seat over the law, mercy covering the broken law, the propitiation, Exodus 25:21, the figure, mercy and truth met, righteousness and peace, the law satisfied by the propitiation, Psalm 85:10; Romans 3:25, the providence, the law within, mercy above, the figure of the propitiation over the law) — Clarke, Gill.

themes: And he took and put the testimony into the ark, and set the staves on the ark, and put the mercy seat above upon the ark
the placing of the testimony in the ark and the mercy seat above
the law within, covered by the mercy seat, mercy over the broken law (Exodus 25:16, 21)
mercy and truth met in the propitiation (Psalm 85:10; Romans 3:25)

schools.summary: The ark set in the most holy place behind the veil: 'and he brought the ark into the tabernacle, and set up the vail of the covering, and covered the ark of the testimony; as the LORD commanded Moses' — Moses bringing the ark into the most holy place, hanging the veil, and screening the ark, as commanded (the ark, set in the most holy place, the throne of God, the veil, hung up, dividing the holy from the most holy, screening the ark, Exodus 26:33, the figure, the ark hidden behind the veil, the holiest not yet open, Hebrews 9:8, the veil rent in Christ, Hebrews 10:19-20); the ark set in the holiest and the veil hung (the most holy place screened, Exodus 26:33) — the figure (the way into the holiest not yet manifest, Hebrews 9:8); 'covered the ark of the testimony; as the LORD commanded Moses' (the ark screened, the holiest hidden, the obedience repeated, 'as the LORD commanded,' the figure, the holiest opened in Christ, the veil rent, Hebrews 10:19-20, the providence, the ark set and veiled, the work done as commanded) — Keil and Delitzsch, Gill.

themes: And he brought the ark into the tabernacle, and set up the vail of the covering, and covered the ark of the testimony; as the LORD commanded Moses
the ark set in the most holy place behind the veil
the holiest furniture set and screened by the veil (Exodus 26:33)
the figure of the hidden holiest opened in Christ (Hebrews 9:8; 10:19-20)

schools.summary: The setting of the table in the holy place: 'and he put the table in the tent of the congregation, upon the side of the tabernacle northward, without the vail' — Moses setting the table (of shewbread) in the holy place, on the north side, outside the veil (the table, the table of the bread of the Presence, Exodus 25:23-30, the position, the north side of the holy place, without the veil, the figure, the table of the Presence, Christ the bread of life, John 6:35, the bread before God); the table set in the holy place (the bread of the Presence, Exodus 26:35) — the figure (Christ the bread, John 6:35); 'upon the side of the tabernacle northward, without the vail' (the table on the north side, outside the veil, the holy place, Exodus 26:35, the figure, the bread of the Presence before God, the providence, the table set, the figure of Christ the bread) — Ellicott, Gill.

themes: And he put the table in the tent of the congregation, upon the side of the tabernacle northward, without the vail
the setting of the table in the holy place
the table of the Presence set in the holy place (Exodus 25:23-30; 26:35)
the figure of Christ the bread of life (John 6:35)

schools.summary: The setting of the shewbread in order before the LORD: 'and he set the bread in order upon it before the LORD; as the LORD had commanded Moses' — Moses setting the shewbread (the bread of the Presence) in order on the table, before the LORD, as commanded (the bread, the twelve loaves, the bread of the Presence, the tribes before God, Leviticus 24:5-8, the order, set in rows, before the LORD, the figure, the tribes ever before God, Christ the bread of life, the communion, John 6:35; 1 Corinthians 10:17); the shewbread set in order (the bread of the Presence, before the LORD, Leviticus 24:6) — the figure (the tribes before God, the bread the figure of Christ); 'as the LORD had commanded Moses' (the refrain of exact obedience, the bread set as commanded, the figure, the faithful obedience, the bread before God, the providence, the shewbread set, the obedience repeated, the tribes before God) — Keil and Delitzsch, Gill.

themes: And he set the bread in order upon it before the LORD; as the LORD had commanded Moses
the setting of the shewbread in order before the LORD
the tribes ever before God, the bread of the Presence (Leviticus 24:5-8)
the figure of Christ the bread of life, the work done as commanded (John 6:35)

schools.summary: The setting of the candlestick in the holy place: 'and he put the candlestick in the tent of the congregation, over against the table, on the side of the tabernacle southward' — Moses setting the candlestick (the golden lampstand) in the holy place, opposite the table, on the south side (the candlestick, the light of the holy place, Exodus 25:31-40, the position, the south side, over against the table on the north, the figure, the light of the Spirit, the church the light-bearer, Revelation 1:20, the light over against the bread, the Spirit and the word together); the candlestick set in the holy place (the light, Exodus 26:35) — the figure (the light of the Spirit, the church the light, Matthew 5:14-16); 'over against the table, on the side of the tabernacle southward' (the candlestick opposite the table, the light over against the bread, Exodus 26:35, the figure, the light and the bread together, the Spirit and the word, the providence, the candlestick set, the figure of the light of the Spirit and the church) — Ellicott, Gill.

themes: And he put the candlestick in the tent of the congregation, over against the table, on the side of the tabernacle southward
the setting of the candlestick in the holy place
the light of the holy place set opposite the bread (Exodus 25:31-40; 26:35)
the figure of the light of the Spirit and the church the light-bearer (Revelation 1:20)

schools.summary: The lighting of the lamps before the LORD: 'and he lighted the lamps before the LORD; as the LORD commanded Moses' — Moses lighting the lamps of the candlestick before the LORD, as commanded (the lamps, the seven lights of the candlestick lit, Exodus 25:37, the light, burning before the LORD, the continual light of the holy place, the figure, the light of testimony, the church holding forth the light, Philippians 2:15-16, the light kept burning before God); the lamps lit before the LORD (the continual light, Exodus 27:20-21) — the figure (the light burning before God, the testimony, the church the light); 'as the LORD commanded Moses' (the refrain of exact obedience, the lamps lit as commanded, the figure, the faithful obedience, the light kept as God appointed, the providence, the lamps lit, the obedience repeated, the light burning before God) — JFB, Gill.

themes: And he lighted the lamps before the LORD; as the LORD commanded Moses
the lighting of the lamps before the LORD
the light of the holy place burning before God (Exodus 25:37; 27:20-21)
the figure of the church holding forth the light (Philippians 2:15-16)

schools.summary: The setting of the golden altar before the veil: 'and he put the golden altar in the tent of the congregation before the vail' — Moses setting the golden altar of incense in the holy place, before the veil (before the ark beyond it, the golden altar, the altar of incense, before the veil, Exodus 30:6, the position, before the veil, nearest the most holy place, the place of prayer before the throne, the figure, the altar of prayer before the throne of God, the intercession, Hebrews 7:25; Revelation 8:3-4); the golden altar set before the veil (the altar of incense, Exodus 30:6) — the figure (prayer ascending before God, Psalm 141:2); 'before the vail' (the altar before the veil, nearest the throne, the place of prayer before God, Exodus 30:6, the figure, the altar of prayer before the throne, Christ's intercession before God, Hebrews 7:25; Revelation 8:3-4, the providence, the golden altar set, the figure of prayer and intercession) — Ellicott, Gill.

themes: And he put the golden altar in the tent of the congregation before the vail
the setting of the golden altar before the veil
the altar of incense before the throne, the place of prayer (Exodus 30:6; Psalm 141:2)
the figure of prayer and Christ's intercession (Hebrews 7:25; Revelation 8:3-4)

schools.summary: The burning of the incense on the golden altar: 'and he burnt sweet incense thereon; as the LORD commanded Moses' — Moses burning the sweet incense on the golden altar, as commanded (the incense, the sweet incense burned, the figure of prayer, Exodus 30:7-8, the burning, the incense ascending before God, the figure, prayer ascending to God, the prayers of the saints, Psalm 141:2; Revelation 5:8; 8:3-4, the incense perfumed by Christ's merit); the sweet incense burned (the figure of prayer, Exodus 30:7) — the figure (prayer ascending, the prayers of the saints, Revelation 8:3-4); 'as the LORD commanded Moses' (the refrain of exact obedience, the incense burned as commanded, the figure, the prayer offered as God appointed, not strange fire, Leviticus 10:1, the providence, the incense burned, the obedience repeated, prayer ascending before God) — Ellicott, Gill.

themes: And he burnt sweet incense thereon; as the LORD commanded Moses
the burning of the incense on the golden altar
the incense ascending before God, the figure of prayer (Exodus 30:7-8)
the prayers of the saints, the work done as commanded (Revelation 8:3-4)

schools.summary: The setting up of the door hanging: 'and he set up the hanging at the door of the tabernacle' — Moses setting up the hanging (screen) at the door (entrance) of the tabernacle, the entrance to the holy place (the hanging, the screen at the door of the holy place, Exodus 26:36, the door, the entrance to the holy place, where the priests entered, the figure, the entrance to the holy place, the access by way of the appointed door); the hanging set up (the screen at the door, Exodus 26:36) — the entrance (the way into the holy place); 'at the door of the tabernacle' (the screen at the door, the entrance, Exodus 26:36, the figure, the door, the appointed way of entrance, Christ the door, John 10:9, the providence, the door hanging set, the entrance to the holy place) — Ellicott, Gill.

themes: And he set up the hanging at the door of the tabernacle
the setting up of the door hanging
the screen at the entrance to the holy place (Exodus 26:36)
the figure of the appointed way of access, Christ the door (John 10:9)

schools.summary: The setting of the altar of burnt offering and the offerings upon it: 'and he put the altar of burnt offering by the door of the tabernacle of the tent of the congregation, and offered upon it the burnt offering and the meat offering; as the LORD commanded Moses' — Moses setting the brazen altar at the entrance of the court, and offering the burnt offering and meat (meal) offering upon it, as commanded (the altar, the place of sacrifice at the door, Exodus 27:1-8, the offerings, the burnt offering and meal offering, the first sacrifices offered, the figure, the altar of sacrifice, the cross, the offerings, the figure of Christ's self-offering, Ephesians 5:2; Hebrews 10:10); the altar set at the door (the place of sacrifice, vv.6) — the figure (the cross, the way of approach); 'offered upon it the burnt offering and the meat offering; as the LORD commanded Moses' (the offerings made, the burnt and meal offerings, the figures of consecration and thanksgiving, the figure, Christ the burnt offering wholly given, Ephesians 5:2, the providence, the altar set and the offerings made, the work done as commanded) — Ellicott, Gill.

themes: And he put the altar of burnt offering by the door of the tabernacle of the tent of the congregation, and offered upon it the burnt offering and the meat offering; as the LORD commanded Moses
the setting of the altar of burnt offering and the offerings upon it
the place of sacrifice at the door, the offerings made (Exodus 27:1-8)
the figure of the cross and Christ's self-offering (Ephesians 5:2; Hebrews 10:10)

schools.summary: The setting of the laver and the water for washing: 'and he set the laver between the tent of the congregation and the altar, and put water there, to wash withal' — Moses setting the laver between the tabernacle and the altar, and putting water in it for washing (the laver, the basin for the priests' washing, Exodus 30:18-21, the position, between the altar and the tent, the washing after sacrifice, before approach, the water, for the cleansing of the priests, the figure, the washing after atonement, the cleansing of those who approach to serve, Titus 3:5; Hebrews 10:22); the laver between the altar and the tent (the washing after the sacrifice, Exodus 30:18) — the order (the sacrifice, then the washing, then the approach); 'put water there, to wash withal' (the water for washing, the cleansing of the priests, the figure, the washing of water by the word, the cleansing of those who serve, Ephesians 5:26; Hebrews 10:22, the providence, the laver and water set, the figure of cleansing) — Keil and Delitzsch, Gill.

themes: And he set the laver between the tent of the congregation and the altar, and put water there, to wash withal
the setting of the laver and the water for washing
the washing after the atonement, before the approach (Exodus 30:18-21)
the cleansing of those who approach to serve God (Hebrews 10:22)

schools.summary: Moses, Aaron, and his sons washing at the laver: 'and Moses and Aaron and his sons washed their hands and their feet thereat' — Moses, Aaron, and his sons washing their hands and feet at the laver (the cleansing before the service, Exodus 30:19-21, the washing, the hands and feet washed, the cleansing of those who minister, the figure, the cleansing of those who serve God, the daily washing of the walk and work, the feet washed, John 13:10, 'he that is washed needeth not save to wash his feet,' the necessity, no service without the washing, the priests cleansed before approach); Moses, Aaron, and his sons washing (the cleansing of those who minister, Exodus 30:19) — the figure (the cleansing of God's servants before the service); 'their hands and their feet thereat' (the hands and feet washed, the cleansing of the work and the walk, the figure, the daily cleansing of those already washed, John 13:10, the feet washed by Christ, the providence, the priests washed, the cleansing before the service) — JFB, Gill.

themes: And Moses and Aaron and his sons washed their hands and their feet thereat
Moses, Aaron, and his sons washing at the laver
the cleansing of those who minister, before the service (Exodus 30:19-21)
the daily washing of the walk and work (John 13:10)

schools.summary: The constant washing before service: 'when they went into the tent of the congregation, and when they came near unto the altar, they washed; as the LORD commanded Moses' — whenever the priests went into the tent or came near the altar, they washing, as commanded (the constant washing, the washing required each time, before every service, Exodus 30:20, the necessity, no approach without the washing, on pain of death, the figure, the continual need of cleansing in approaching God, the daily washing, 1 John 1:7-9, the holiness required of those who serve); the priests washing each time they served (the constant cleansing, Exodus 30:20) — the necessity (no service without the washing); 'as the LORD commanded Moses' (the refrain of exact obedience, the washing as commanded, the figure, the continual cleansing required, the holiness of God's service, the providence, the constant washing, the obedience repeated, the holiness required in approaching God) — Gill.

themes: When they went into the tent of the congregation, and when they came near unto the altar, they washed; as the LORD commanded Moses
the constant washing before service
the continual cleansing required in approaching God (Exodus 30:20)
the holiness of God's service, the daily washing (1 John 1:7-9)

schools.summary: The finishing of the work of the tabernacle: 'and he reared up the court round about the tabernacle and the altar, and set up the hanging of the court gate. So Moses finished the work' — Moses setting up the court and its gate, and so finishing all the work of the tabernacle (the court, the enclosure round the tabernacle and altar, the last part set up, the finishing, the work completed, all done as commanded, the echo, the finishing of the work echoes the finishing of creation, 'thus the heavens and the earth were finished... and God ended his work,' Genesis 2:1-2, the tabernacle a new creation, God's dwelling); the court set up (the enclosure, the last part, Exodus 27:9) — the completion (the work nearly done); 'So Moses finished the work' (the work completed, all the work of the tabernacle finished, as commanded, the figure — the echo of creation finished, Genesis 2:1-2, 'God ended his work which he had made,' the tabernacle a new creation, God's dwelling reared, and the figure of the finished work of redemption, 'It is finished,' John 19:30, the work of God's house, the redemption, completed, the providence, Moses finishing the work, the dwelling complete, ready for the glory) — Ellicott, Gill.

themes: And he reared up the court round about the tabernacle and the altar, and set up the hanging of the court gate. So Moses finished the work
the finishing of the work of the tabernacle
the work of God's dwelling completed as commanded, the echo of creation finished (Genesis 2:1-2)
the dwelling ready for the glory, a figure of the finished work (John 19:30)

schools.summary: The cloud and the glory of the LORD filling the tabernacle: 'then a cloud covered the tent of the congregation, and the glory of the LORD filled the tabernacle' — the climax — 'a cloud covered the tent of the congregation, and the glory of the LORD filled the tabernacle': when the work was finished, the cloud (the visible token of God's presence) covering the tent, and the glory of the LORD (the shekinah, the radiant manifestation of his presence) filling the tabernacle (the witnesses noting the significance — God taking possession of his house, the great purpose accomplished, God now dwelling in the midst of his people, Exodus 25:8, 'that I may dwell among them,' the cloud and the glory, the same cloud and glory of Sinai, Exodus 24:16-17, now come down to dwell, the pillar of cloud now resting on the tabernacle, the lesson — the reward of obedience, the work done as commanded, God filling it with his glory, the figure — God dwelling with man, the glory in the tabernacle, the figure of the incarnation, 'the Word... dwelt among us, and we beheld his glory,' John 1:14, the glory filling the temple, 1 Kings 8:10-11; 2 Chronicles 7:1-2, the glory of God in the church, Ephesians 2:21-22, and the consummation, the glory of God lightening the new Jerusalem, Revelation 21:3, 11, 23, the presence of God the crown of all the work); the glory filling the tabernacle (God taking possession, the dwelling now his) — the indwelling (God dwelling among his people, the great end of the tabernacle, the presence the glory of the house, Haggai 2:7-9, the figure, God dwelling with man, fulfilled in Christ and in the church); the cloud and the glory — the visible presence of God taking possession (the providence, God filling his house with his glory, the dwelling now his dwelling-place) — Calvin, Wesley, Henry, Gill.

debates.question: What is the significance of the glory filling the tabernacle?

sides.position: God taking possession of his house, the great purpose accomplished (Exodus 25:8), the cloud and glory of Sinai (Exodus 24:16-17) come down to dwell - a figure of God dwelling with man, the incarnation ('the Word dwelt among us, and we beheld his glory,' John 1:14), the glory filling the temple (1 Kings 8:10-11), the church (Ephesians 2:21-22), and the new Jerusalem (Revelation 21:3, 23)

themes: Then a cloud covered the tent of the congregation, and the glory of the LORD filled the tabernacle
the cloud and the glory of the LORD filling the tabernacle (the crux of God's presence taking possession of his house)
God taking possession of his dwelling, dwelling in the midst of his people (Exodus 25:8; 24:16-17)
a figure of God dwelling with man in Christ (John 1:14; 1 Kings 8:10-11; Revelation 21:3, 23)

schools.summary: Moses unable to enter because of the glory: 'and Moses was not able to enter into the tent of the congregation, because the cloud abode thereon, and the glory of the LORD filled the tabernacle' — even Moses (the mediator, who had entered the cloud on Sinai, Exodus 24:18) could not enter the tent, because the cloud rested on it and the glory filled it (the inability, the glory so great that even Moses could not enter, the cloud abiding, the presence resting, dwelling, not passing, the figure, the overwhelming glory of God, before which even the mediator must stand back, the holiness of God's presence); Moses unable to enter because of the glory (the witnesses noting the lesson — the awful, overwhelming holiness of God's presence, the glory too great for man to enter, even Moses, the holiness before which man must stand back, as the priests could not stand to minister when the glory filled the temple, 1 Kings 8:11; 2 Chronicles 5:14, the figure, the unapproachable glory, the holiness of God, the access not yet fully open, Hebrews 9:8, yet in Christ the access made, the glory beheld with open face, 2 Corinthians 3:18, the new and living way, Hebrews 10:19-20, the greater glory of the gospel, the glory once unapproachable, now in Christ the way opened); the glory filling the tabernacle — the overwhelming presence (the providence, the glory so full that Moses could not enter, the holiness of God's presence, the access opened later in Christ) — Keil and Delitzsch, Gill.

debates.question: Why could Moses not enter the tabernacle?

sides.position: The overwhelming, unapproachable holiness of God's presence (even the mediator unable to enter, as the priests at the temple, 1 Kings 8:11) - the figure of the access not yet fully open (Hebrews 9:8), yet opened in Christ, the glory beheld with open face and the new way made (2 Corinthians 3:18; Hebrews 10:19-20)

themes: And Moses was not able to enter into the tent of the congregation, because the cloud abode thereon, and the glory of the LORD filled the tabernacle
Moses unable to enter because of the glory
the overwhelming, unapproachable holiness of God's presence (1 Kings 8:11)
the access not yet fully open (Hebrews 9:8), yet opened in Christ (2 Corinthians 3:18; Hebrews 10:19-20)

schools.summary: The cloud taken up as the signal to journey: 'and when the cloud was taken up from over the tabernacle, the children of Israel went onward in all their journeys' — when the cloud (the visible presence of God) was taken up from the tabernacle, Israel journeying onward (the cloud, the pillar of cloud, the guide of the people, Numbers 9:17-23, the signal, the cloud rising the signal to move, the cloud resting the signal to stay, the guidance, the people led by the cloud, following the presence of God); the cloud taken up (the signal to journey, Numbers 9:17) — the guidance (the people following the cloud, led by God's presence); 'the children of Israel went onward in all their journeys' (the people journeying when the cloud rose, the guidance of God through all their journeys, the figure, the people led by God's presence, the guidance of the Spirit, Romans 8:14, the pilgrim people following God, the providence, the cloud the guide, the people led through the wilderness) — Clarke, Gill.

themes: And when the cloud was taken up from over the tabernacle, the children of Israel went onward in all their journeys
the cloud taken up as the signal to journey
the people led by the cloud, following the presence of God (Numbers 9:17-23)
the figure of the people led by God's presence and the Spirit (Romans 8:14)

schools.summary: The cloud resting as the signal to stay: 'but if the cloud were not taken up, then they journeyed not till the day that it was taken up' — if the cloud did not rise, the people not journeying, but staying until it was taken up (the resting, the cloud resting the signal to stay, Numbers 9:19-22, the waiting, the people waiting on the cloud, not moving without it, the obedience, the people following the cloud exactly, going only when it rose, staying when it rested, the figure, the people waiting on God's guidance, not running before him, following his leading, Psalm 32:8); the people not journeying without the cloud (waiting on God's signal, Numbers 9:19) — the obedience (the people following God's guidance, not moving without him); 'till the day that it was taken up' (the people waiting until the cloud rose, however long, the waiting on God, the figure, the patience of following God's leading, waiting on his time, not running before him, Isaiah 40:31; Psalm 27:14, the providence, the people led by the cloud, waiting and moving at God's signal) — JFB, Gill.

themes: But if the cloud were not taken up, then they journeyed not till the day that it was taken up
the cloud resting as the signal to stay
the people waiting on God's guidance, not running before him (Numbers 9:19-22)
the patience of following God's leading, waiting on his time (Psalm 27:14; Isaiah 40:31)

schools.summary: The cloud by day and fire by night through all their journeys: 'for the cloud of the LORD was upon the tabernacle by day, and fire was on it by night, in the sight of all the house of Israel, throughout all their journeys' — the abiding presence — 'the cloud of the LORD was upon the tabernacle by day, and fire was on it by night, in the sight of all the house of Israel, throughout all their journeys': the cloud resting on the tabernacle by day and appearing as fire by night, visible to all Israel, through all their journeys to the promised land (the witnesses noting the significance — the abiding presence of God with his people, the cloud and fire, the visible token of God dwelling and going with them, day and night, Exodus 13:21-22; Numbers 9:15-16, the guidance, the presence both their guide and their assurance, leading them through the wilderness, the lesson — the constant presence of God with his pilgrim people, never leaving them, by day and night, through all their journeys, 'I will never leave thee, nor forsake thee,' Hebrews 13:5; Matthew 28:20, 'lo, I am with you alway,' the figure — God's abiding presence with the church, the light and guide of his people to the end, the Lord their everlasting light, Isaiah 60:19-20, the glory and the Lamb the light of the new Jerusalem, Revelation 21:23; 22:5, the close of the book on the assured presence of God dwelling with and guiding his redeemed people); the cloud and fire visible to all, through all their journeys (the assurance of God's presence to the whole people, to the end) — the comfort (the visible, constant presence, the guide and assurance of the pilgrim people, to the promised land); the cloud by day, the fire by night — the abiding presence and guidance (the providence, God's presence with his people through all their journeys, the book closing on the indwelling, guiding presence) — Calvin, Clarke, Gill.

debates.question: What does the cloud by day and fire by night signify?

sides.position: The abiding presence of God with his pilgrim people, both guide and assurance, day and night, to the promised land (Exodus 13:21-22; Numbers 9:15-16) - a figure of God's constant presence with his church ('I will never leave thee,' Hebrews 13:5; Matthew 28:20) and the Lord the everlasting light and guide of his people (Isaiah 60:19-20; Revelation 21:23)

themes: For the cloud of the LORD was upon the tabernacle by day, and fire was on it by night, in the sight of all the house of Israel, throughout all their journeys
the cloud by day and fire by night through all their journeys (the crux of the abiding presence and guidance)
the abiding presence of God with his pilgrim people, guide and assurance (Exodus 13:21-22; Numbers 9:15-16)
a figure of God's constant presence with his church to the end (Hebrews 13:5; Matthew 28:20; Revelation 21:23)
